summary: Iteration 214 — Proposal PDF font consistency fix validated. Backend 5/5 pytest pass (3.4s). Verified: (1) Setting title.font + every section heading_font/body_font to 'helvetica' → generated PDF embeds Helvetica AND Helvetica-Bold (proves date/heading/disclaimer/header-footer now honor the template font). (2) Same for 'times' → Times-Roman + Times-Bold present, Helvetica-Bold absent (lone 'Helvetica' baseline tolerated as ReportLab artifact). (3) Per-lead override carrying ₹ in paragraph + pricing disclaimer with font=helvetica → generation succeeds AND DejaVuSans appears (for the rupee glyph only) alongside Helvetica*. (4) Footer 'Page {n} of {total}' + font=times → two-pass generation produces valid %PDF- with Times-Roman/Times-Bold, no Helvetica-Bold leakage. (5) Regression: rich-text content (<strong>/<em>/<ul>) + plain text with '&' still generates valid %PDF-. Frontend: /proposal-template Title card exposes 'Use this font for the whole proposal' button (data-testid='apply-font-all-btn'); set title font to Helvetica via the title font picker, clicked apply-font-all-btn (toast 'Font applied to all sections — remember to Save'), clicked save-template-btn (toast 'Proposal template saved'), and a subsequent GET /api/proposals/templates/{id} confirmed title.font='helvetica' and ALL 8 sections (paragraph, pricing_table, 4×list, category, image) have heading_font='helvetica' AND body_font='helvetica'.
critical_code_review_comments: _needs_unicode() checks for ANY non-Latin-1 codepoint (>255). This correctly flags ₹ (U+20B9) but ALSO flags common typographic dashes — (U+2014) and – (U+2013) which Helvetica/Times CAN render via WinAnsiEncoding mappings (0x96/0x97). As a result, the default template's prose containing em/en-dashes and the lead's SKU names like 'Nyla – 600 ml / Silver' force DejaVu fallback for those lines even when the user picked Helvetica/Times. This is benign (still produces a valid PDF) but slightly defeats the 'use Helvetica everywhere' intent. Consider tightening _needs_unicode to only catch the small set of glyphs the standard 14 PDF base fonts genuinely cannot encode (e.g. ₹ U+20B9, ₿, U+2192→ if used) — or whitelist Latin-1-supplement + General-Punctuation dashes/quotes via a per-font glyph table.

_smart_font is applied per-text-fragment in pricing cells and header/footer lines (good — minimizes DejaVu pollution). Verified by the fact that Helvetica-Bold IS present in font=helvetica runs (heading & bold-cell text honors the font).

applyFontToAll only mutates client state and shows a toast 'remember to Save' — does NOT auto-save. The button correctly updates title.font + every section's heading_font & body_font in a single setTpl call. Confirmed live: 8/8 sections updated and persisted on subsequent Save.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (apply-font-all-btn click → toast → save → API confirms all 8 sections honour title font)

test_credentials: surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (tenant: nyla-air-water, role: CEO). Login returns session_token (Bearer); frontend uses cookie session.
seed_data_creation: Default template content was mutated mid-run (PUT with helvetica/times). After all tests + frontend run, restored Default template to FACTORY defaults via PUT with body=GET response's 'defaults' object (title font now back to 'dejavu', all 8 sections back to dejavu/dejavu). Per-lead override on 08c93122-99fc-4587-b31c-559649f29c17 cleared via DELETE.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 214 backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_214_proposal_font_consistency.py — 5/5 green. To inspect embedded BaseFonts in generated PDFs: base64.b64decode the /api/leads/{id}/proposal/download response's proposal.file_data, regex /BaseFont\s*/([A-Za-z0-9+\-]+). Note: a lone 'Helvetica' BaseFont is a ReportLab baseline artifact even for Times PDFs — treat as non-issue. DejaVuSans will appear whenever the rendered text contains characters with codepoint >255 (₹, em-dash '—', en-dash '–', arrow '→', bullet '•', box-drawing '─'). The default template & the test lead's SKU name 'Nyla – 600 ml / Silver' both contain en/em dashes, so DejaVuSans appears even with no ₹. Frontend selectors: data-testid='apply-font-all-btn', 'save-template-btn', 'tpl-title-card'. Title font select is the FIRST [role='combobox'] inside tpl-title-card.
rca of the issue: Iteration 214 fix RCA: BEFORE fix, date paragraph, pricing-table cells, pricing disclaimer, and header/footer text in proposal_pdf.py were hardcoded to 'DejaVu' (FONTS['dejavu']) regardless of template.title.font / section.body_font — so a user-chosen Helvetica template still rendered most of the page in DejaVu. FIX (this iteration): introduced _smart_font(key, text, bold) which returns the requested font's regular/bold name UNLESS the text contains non-Latin-1 glyphs, in which case it falls back to DejaVu only for that specific text fragment. Applied in (a) date_s and small ParagraphStyles via _font(base_font_key) using title.font as document base; (b) pricing-table pcell + head_style — per-cell Paragraphs built with _smart_font(body_font, text, bold); (c) _draw_zone now accepts base_font and uses _smart_font per line for header/footer text. Frontend added applyFontToAll(font) helper + apply-font-all-btn so users can propagate the title font to every section in one click. Validation confirms: with font=helvetica/times set everywhere, Helvetica-Bold/Times-Bold appears in the embedded BaseFonts (proving heading/bold paths now honor the chosen font), and the inverse (Helvetica-Bold in Times PDFs, or Times in Helvetica PDFs) does NOT appear.
